entry.title: Re: Not able to save in Content Server (Log Full issue)
entry.content: I have no knowledge in DMS or SAP systems in general, but I know from MaxDB that a Log Full issue usually happens if somehow the backup of MaxDB is not working. If you're using MaxDB then you may want to check it.

entry.title: Re: SPAU TR not given to SUM tool
entry.content: in QAS, stms import the tr, that should be the same.

entry.title: Re: How to assign a role pragmatically to a user in SAP B...
entry.content:
To assign a role programmatically to a user in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), you can follow these steps:

1. Define Roles: First, define the roles you want to assign to users in your SAP BTP account. Roles can be defined in the SAP BTP cockpit or SAP Identity Authentication service.

2. Create an Access Control Model (ACM): In your CAP project, define an Access Control Model where you specify the roles and permissions for your application. This can be done in the access folder of your CAP project.

3. Implement Authorization Logic: Implement the necessary logic to determine which roles should be assigned to users. This logic could be based on user attributes, such as user type, department, or any other relevant criteria.

4. Assign Roles: Use the SAP BTP APIs or SDKs within your CAP project to assign roles to users programmatically. You can leverage the User and Role Management APIs provided by SAP BTP for this purpose.

5. Testing and Validation: Test your implementation thoroughly to ensure that roles are being assigned correctly based on your authorization logic.

Here's a general example of how you might implement role assignment in CAP:

// Import necessary modules
const xsenv = require('@sap/xsenv');
const { User, Role } = require('@sap/hana-client');

// Get SAP BTP service credentials
const credentials = xsenv.getServices({ xsuaa: { tag: 'xsuaa' } }).xsuaa;

// Function to assign role to user
async function assignRoleToUser(userId, roleId) {
// Connect to SAP HANA to manage roles
const conn = xsenv.getHanaOptions().connection;

// Create user and role objects
const user = new User(conn);
const role = new Role(conn);

try {
// Begin transaction
conn.exec('BEGIN');

// Check if user exists
const userExists = await user.exists(userId);
if (!userExists) {
// Handle user not found
console.error(`User ${userId} not found.`);
return;
}

// Check if role exists
const roleExists = await role.exists(roleId);
if (!roleExists) {
// Handle role not found
console.error(`Role ${roleId} not found.`);
return;
}

// Assign role to user
await user.grantRole(userId, roleId);

// Commit transaction
conn.exec('COMMIT');

console.log(`Role ${roleId} assigned to user ${userId} successfully.`);
} catch (error) {
// Rollback transaction in case of error
conn.exec('ROLLBACK');
console.error('Error assigning role:', error);
} finally {
// Close connection
conn.close();
}
}

// Example usage
const userId = 'example_user';
const roleId = 'example_role';
assignRoleToUser(userId, roleId);

Please note that this is a simplified example and you may need to adapt it to fit your specific requirements and environment. Also, make sure to handle errors and edge cases appropriately in your production code.

entry.title: Re: SPAU TR not given to SUM tool
entry.content: After scn site screen upgraded its difficult to me post Q&A, Sorry for that.

entry.title: Solution Manager system copy
entry.content:
Hi team,
We are planning to migrate our Solman 7.2 SP 16 to another Data center and want to check how can we move all the LMDB, SLD configuration and want to understand if there is any guide or the process for the Solman configurations. Please help.
DO we have any tasklist for this? What would be the best practise ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Solution Manager system copy
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Can you elaborate on what you want to migrate on Solman? Is it a system copy or a database migration? 
If it is a database migration, normally the configuration will be kept if you just migrate the database and the data is stored to the new DB successfully.
However if DB SID or host name will be changed after migration, then the original configuration might be affected.

Regarding the SLD migration, please check if Note 2068999 is helpful
##2068999 - Migrating an SLD

If further help is required with database migration/system copy, you can contact BC-INS-MIG.

Best regards,
Leo
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Missing purpose string in Info.plist
entry.content: Is this issue solved?

entry.title: Material advance planning status marked 'Not relevant for planning' after upgrade 2022 version
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have upgraded our system to S4 HANA 2022 version, after upgrade while testing we observed in material advance planning tab indictor marked and status field newly added with description 'Not relevant for planning' but same upgrade we did in out test system there working fine. kindly help me if anybody faced same issues or any lead which will great help full.

Thanks.

entry.title: Audit Management SAP QM Public cloud

entry.title: Cost component split when price control V
entry.content:
Hi gurus,

a bit unusual question probably, but I failed to discover an exact answer - I have a material managed as MAP (price control=V, price determination=2) which doesn't make it possible to record a standard cost estimate at the material level, even if the one is calculated (it remains as 'wo material reference' which is an expected system behavior). I'm thinking of switching the price determination to 3 instead of 2, with price control still V, just in order to switch the material ledger tables in, and have a chance to have a cost estimate assigned to a material and cost component split available for it. What do you think? What are possible cons to this solution, in terms of stock  valuation in particular?  

@all

Pretty much aware this is NOT a recommended way to manage materials, pls don't spend your time pointing this out. I need a reasonable feedback on this particular approach constraints.

 

Thank you!

entry.title: Create/Update listing exclusion in ECC using PRICAT Inbound IDOC
entry.content:
Requirement is to get listing/exclusion maintained automatically in system, upon receiving PRICAT IDOC in SAP ECC system.

Kindly help if its achievable in standard and please share related Notes/Link/steps to do it.

Thanks!

entry.title: SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation: List Management
entry.content:
Greetings,

The purpose of the post is to explain, seek advise and robust solutions to the below Anomaly experienced when populating a List Variable in SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation.

Use case:

We are currently automating a propose by reading data from a 3rd party solution using a REST API.

Figure 1: Process Automation

  1. We have a Custom script embedded with the API logic
  2. Call web service element for the service call 
  3. Write to Json file element: which holds the returned results from the webservice 
  4. Read json file element: to read a specific field being the username within the json file
  5. For Each element: within the for each loop we read a specific field being the username into a list variable and an add item(List) element.
    • Custom script: Which declares an empty list then populates the list with usernames from the previously collected data which resides in the Read json file element step 4

Figure 2:Custom Script Logic

  • Add item(List): Which adds the collected names  in above custom script to the element.

The Above process successfully executes and iterates through the file as expected, However:

  1.  The List variable, Overwrites the entries populated at each iteration instead of appending 
  2. Furthermore,   the List variable holds the last name then populates null values.

Results from various iterations, we have also attached the list results to the post:

 

Kind Regards,

entry.title: Upload Mass Data for Shared Service Framework Configuration
entry.content:
Hi All,

At the moment maintenance of the SSF MD elements such as routing rules, organizational structure, approval engine requires manual, click-based effort by business administrator. This is time consuming and ineffective both for initial implementation and further maintenance. We are searching for hints/good practices/automations (like upload/download features) that can be applied to limit this unnecessary effort.

We could imagine to have a solution like we have it for Smart Close. Upload/download functions are available in Smart Close. You can create a download (to excel file) from the certain feature/functionality/configuration of the Smart Close, adjust the data/ add rows etc. and upload this file back to the system. This works great when you need to do a setup of new Company Code, add users, change certain elements of the month end scenarios etc.

Do you have an idea if this would be possible to have such a solution or if there is already something existing what could be used?

 

Many Thanks!

Alexander

entry.title: Re: Handling Cutoff Dates during BW transition to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you for your time on reading this blog.

I tend to agree with you in principal, all solutions which work are acceptable. We can always compare the execution plan to see in which is the gain.

We have to remember that this temporary solution is focused for Dimensions ,  so even for big customers the DataSet cannot be more than couple of millions and Dimensions do not tend to change so dramatically. Ending, we can always materialize the Result Set either in memory or to a target table.

Thank you once more.

entry.title: Re: Missing purpose string in Info.plist

entry.title: Managing Finite multiple Quantities in Materials\BOM (+ CK11N)
entry.content:
Dear SAP community,

I'm seeking assistance with managing finite quantities in a Bill of Materials (BOM). I need a way to ensure specific components in the BOM remain as int numbers and are not recalculated when evaluating material usage, similar to the "round to multiple" function in Excel.

Example Scenario:

In my production process, I use 3 drill bits for every 1,000 holes. However, when I evaluate a batch of 1,500 holes using transaction CK11N, SAP calculates a requirement of 4.5 drill bits. Ideally, I'd like it to suggest 6 drill bits. 

Desired Behavior:

I need SAP to consider the drill bits in whole numbers, following a multiple of 3:

  • 3 drill bits for 1,000 holes
  • 6 drill bits for 1,500 holes
  • 9 drill bits for 3,000 holes
  • 12 drill bits for 3,100 holes 

Questions:

  1. Is there a way to configure SAP to handle finite quantities in the BOM with a specific multiple?
  2. Can I set a constraint on a specific material in the BOM to enforce int multiple numbers?
  3. While the MENGE field exists in the BOM, is there a way to utilize it to specify whole numbers for this scenario?

Additional Information to explain the problem:

Drill bit yields vary depending on the material:

  • Steel: 3,000 holes per drill bit
  • Brass: 4,000 holes per drill bit

Thank you for your time 

Best regards,

Luca

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA readiness check: check failure
entry.content:
Namespaces and enhancements in ATC - always fun! This is admittedly one of the most complicated parts of running ATC in a customer system, but let's see:

  1. SAP objects like RSNAST00 appear in your set of checked objects because they are modified or enhanced, and either the enhancement is in the object set, or you have activated that modified objects should be checked (on the "Modified Objects" tab when defining an ATC run series).
  2. When modified or enhanced SAP objects are checked, the ATC uses the so-called "check scope" to determine whether the resulting findings are related to the customer modification or enhancement or not. If not, the finding will not be shown in the result. This scope is essentially a list of namespaces that are owned by the customer, see also note 2215288 which delivers the tool with which you can register namespaces for this scope. By default, all producer namespaces with a valid development license are included in the scope.
  3. The ATC "protects" SAP-owned namespaces with an exclusion list, so that customers cannot register these by accident. Since we know that there are various inconsistencies that can lead to this protection being circumvented (and this has in the past led to confusion with customers who then suddenly got ATC findings for SAP code, which of course they cannot fix), there's an additional check where we refuse to check any object that lies in an SAP-owned namespace if an SAP-owned namespace is in scope, since this would then lead to inconsistent results. The SAP object does not have to lie in the problematic namespace for this to happen. That's the explanation for the "The SAP-owned namespace /0SJ1G/ is in scope. SAP objects cannot be checked in this case." message you get. 
  4. So finally: For some reason, the ATC considers the namespace /0SJ1G/ to be in scope, but simultaneously the system considers this to be an SAP-owned namespace. The solution depends on what this namespace really is: If the namespace is actually an SAP-namespace, you/we need to figure out why it is considered in scope in this customer system (some special partner setup where they actually have a development license? Just inconsistent entries in TRNSPACE? something else?) If the namespace is not an SAP namespace, then it's probably inconsistent entries in the system.

As for the error in ADT: I don't know what "SQL error code 4" for your database is, but the dump should tell you a bit more - it might just be a (temporary) problem with your database instead of anything wrong with the ATC. In any case, this is completely unrelated to the namespace problem, the table mentioned in the error message is for the storage of customized ATC priorities.

entry.title: Re: How to delete an email address from contact in SAP Ma...
entry.content:
Hi Florian

 

You can also use the application jobs "Contacts: Flag Contact IDs for Deletion" and "Contacts: Delete Flagged Contact IDs". To check which origin ID you should select, you can go to "Inspect contact", here you find the respective origin of the E-mail address. In this way all interaction data remains at the contact. 

 

Regards,

Danny

entry.title: Re: sap product master data
entry.content:
Hi,

The price with Price Control V cannot be changed manually. So maybe you need to check if the price is also in your upload data.

entry.title: Re: #COE#RFL01-Withholding Tax not Calculated Automatical...
entry.content: Dear @sumesh_gupta, I've checked this point and everything corresponds to the SAP documentation and also to what you've just told me about the process. I've set custo points 104226 and 104227. I've checked the BP and everything's ok. When I create a supplier invoice, the VAT line is calculated automatically, but the HO WHT line goes up but remains at 0. It doesn't calculate automatically, so I have to enter the amount manually. Have you ever had this problem on S/4hana public cloud?

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Test Automation API GET Request
entry.content:
Hi,

Have you reviewed the Test Automation API documentation in the SAP Help Portal? 
The Query Parameters section provides more information about each of the parameters required in a request, including the "calmTenantLabel" parameter. It doesn't provide any examples, but it should shed some light on using the API.

Please note that this API is used by SAP Cloud ALM to make calls to other applications that support this API. It allows you to perform actions and access resources managed by remote services implementing this API. The target host name and the corresponding security protocol depend on those remote services.

So the API does not work "standalone" with only your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.
The API needs to call into another service (your Test Automation tool) and get data from there.
The Business Accelerator Hub Overview page and the Authorization section of the SAP Help Portal documentation clarifies this further. 

Regards,
John

entry.title: where to check administrative data - Account Group in BP - customer master - s4 hana 2023
entry.content: usually i get the account group of a customer from Extras - administrative data - Account Group or from KNA1 table. but now i want to change the account group. but couldn't able to view account group under extras any other guidelines for this.

entry.title: SAP BTP PostgreSQL Database, use as vectorstore database for AI Embeddings
entry.content:
Introduction

In the ever-evolving landscape of technology, leveraging the power of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and machine learning has become essential for businesses seeking a competitive edge. SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP) offers a robust ecosystem for deploying AI solutions, and one crucial component of this ecosystem is the PostgreSQL Database. This guide will walk you through the process of utilizing a SAP BTP-hosted PostgreSQL Database as a vectorstore database for AI embeddings. By following the steps outlined below, you can harness the capabilities of AI embeddings to enhance your data analytics and decision-making processes.

Pre-requisites:

  • BTP Account (can be trial)
  • BTP PostgreSQL Database which can be assesible by SSH Tunnel
  • Cloud Foundry CLI

Step 1 - Obtain necessary information from BTP Cockpit and CF CLI

Before you can configure your PostgreSQL database, gather essential information from both the BTP Cockpit and the CF CLI. This information includes authorization tokens, Cloud Foundry region details, instance IDs, and database names. These details will be crucial for the subsequent steps.

Step 1.1 - Obtain Authorization token from CF CLI

 

cf oauth-token

 

Step 1.2 - Obtain CloudFoundryRegion from BTP Cockpit

Step 1.3 - Obtain PostgreSQL InstanceID from BTP Cockpit

Step 1.4 - Obtain Database name from BTP Cockpit

Step 2- Activate vector extension for PostgreSQL database hosted on BTP

With the prerequisite information in hand, it's time to activate the vector extension for your PostgreSQL database hosted on SAP BTP. This extension will enable your database to efficiently store and manage AI embeddings. You can activate it using various methods, such as CURL commands or Postman requests, as outlined in the guide. This step is pivotal in preparing your database for AI-related tasks.

To activate vector extension, PostgreSQL version should at least be 13.

 

https://api-backing-services.<CloudFoundryRegion>.data.services.cloud.sap/v1/postgresql-db/instances/<InstanceID>/extensions/vector

 

Activate with CURL

 

curl -X PUT 'https://api-backing-services.<CloudFoundryRegion>.data.services.cloud.sap/v1/postgresql-db/instances/<InstanceID>/extensions/vector' \
--header 'Authorization: <AuthorizationToken>' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{"database": "<DatabaseName>"}'

 

Activate with Postman

Step 3 - Check 'Vector' extension activation on pgAdmin

To connect to your PostgreSQL database, you'll need to establish an SSH tunnel. Follow the instructions outlined in the blog post provided below to open the tunnel.
SAP BTP PostgreSQL Database import to pgAdmin with SSH Tunnel (Local Access) 

Now that your PostgreSQL database is configured as a vectorstore for AI embeddings, you can put it to the test. Additionally with above blog, you learnt how to connect to your PostgreSQL database through an SSH tunnel and interact with the vectorized data.

Conclusion

In conclusion, this guide has equipped you with the knowledge and steps required to harness the power of SAP BTP and PostgreSQL as a vectorstore database for AI embeddings. By following these instructions, you can elevate your data analytics, information retrieval, and decision-making capabilities to new heights. AI embeddings offer a transformative approach to handling unstructured data, enabling you to extract valuable insights and drive innovation within your organization. As technology continues to advance, embracing AI and leveraging robust platforms like SAP BTP becomes imperative for staying competitive in the modern business landscape.

entry.title: Unable to cancel Sales order with delivery status of the item is 'In-process' or 'Finished'
entry.content:
We found that we cannot cancel sales order by select reason for rejection when the  delivery status of the item is 'In-process' or 'Finished'. 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to cancel Sales order with delivery status of...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

Once delivery status of the item is 'In-process' or 'Finished', cancellation of the item is not possible in the Sales Order screen

You can refer to the below KBAs. 

KBA 1755905 - You are not able to Cancel a Sales Order

KBA 2644606 - How to maintain the Reason for Rejection in Project Based Sales Order Item

Thanks & Regards,

Arjun T H

SAP Product Support

entry.title: SAP Connector for .NET Framework 5.0 and higher
entry.content:
Hello,

is there any intentions to release the SAP Connector for the Microsoft .NET Framework 5.0 and higher?

At the moment the SAP Connector is released Microsoft .NET Framework 4.6.2.

Reason for question: At Bertelsmann, we are required to use the Microsoft .NET Framework 5.0 release or higher.

Thanks for any help you can offer. 

Best regards,

Kent Kleinsteuber
Arvato Systems Support

entry.title: Re: How to consume the open OData service in SAP CPI?

I have read around that authentication is not supported or or doesn't work properly, but I would like to understand it better.

Thank you all.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA readiness check: check failure
entry.content:
Thanks for your detailed comment, @BjörnJüliger ! at least your explanations fit to my expectations... 🙂 In my opinion it is an error of the check run which

  1. finds a wrong namespace (/0SJ1G/ is the SAP-namespace "Localization Greece" which has nothing to do with the SAP objects found)
  2. does not recognize that the namespace is an SAP-namespace

Concerning the eclipse error: I aggree that this probably was a one time error due to some temporarily system incosistencies.

entry.title: Same Counter for two nodes in target structure
entry.content:
Dear integration fellows,

I have duplicated the subtree in the target structure. So I have now on the target structure twice the node called DetaglioLinee. Because we need to map different fields from source to target. We have in the node DetaglioLinee a field called NumeroLinea. For each line item the counter increases by 1.

Now we have two subtrees with different mapping, but I want the counter to be the same. Is this possible? Can we copy maybe the occurence of the source field mapped to first subtree and increment it by then? (e.g E1EDP01 is occuring 2 times, save 2 in variable and increment by 1)

entry.title: Re: Reject Purchase Requisition Item, just like FIORI does!
entry.content:

Thank you for your article. Btw currently I'm developing own list for approving and rejecting the PR and PO. While I copy and paste your code, I got the message that rejection not allowed. I tried debugging your code but I seems lost. Please advise what's the  condition that trigger the message "rejection not allowed" ?

Thank you,

Best Regards,

Afin

entry.title: AI/ML apps and Visual inspection APIs Deprecated
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am creating AI/ML scenarios using the Manage AI/ML Scenarios app and monitoring the inspection results in the Manage Analytical Data Sets app. I also wanted to record visual inspection data using the available APIs. But I came across the following announcements - 


I also found the following on Roadmap Explorer-

My Question - As the APIs and services have been deprecated and based on the announcements the two apps will also be deprecated, before Q4 2024 and Q1 2025 updates which apps should we use for the AI/ML scenario building and what APIs to use? Do we have to use the same apps and APIs available till the updates or other changes will also come before the scheduled updates on the roadmap?

PS: My requirement was to remove "Capture and Inspect" in assisted mode for Visual Inspection and enable auto-capture and recording of results. If anyone can please recommend a way to achieve this, it would be very helpful.

Thanks and Regards,
Harsh.

entry.title: augment_update Method not called
entry.content:
Hi experts!

I have a RAP App on the BTP where I want use augmentation for update. The problem is, that the method is not called.

The implementation is

Projection BDEF 

use update (augment);

Projection Implementation

METHODS augment_update FOR MODIFY
  IMPORTING entities FOR UPDATE DemoArticle.

Szenario: Managed, OData V4

Does anyone knows, why the implementation is not called?

Thanks and regards, Sebastian

entry.title: SAP CPI OAuth2 Authorization Code (Generic) The Provider 'Generic' did not return a refresh token
entry.content:
hi,

on cpi I configured an OAuth2 Authorization Code (Generic) user in the security material

 

but when I click on the "authorize" button I receive this error The Provider 'Generic' did not return a refresh token for the OAuth2 Authorization Code Credential

can someone help me?

entry.title: Error deleting rows in local table with data editor
entry.content:
I am having a problem trying to delete rows from a local table with the data editor.
I always get INTERNAL SERVER ERROR.

After some research, I think the problem is that the table has a field with a technical name that is "GROUP", and I guess that the part of the program that does the DELETE, does not escape the names of the fields with double quotes and being GROUP a reserved word in SQL the DELETE FROM T WHERE GROUP = 1 statement fails.
update: well, a DELETE with a field named "VALUE" works great, so seems that the problem is just with the name GROUP. 

If you encounter this error, maybe it will help you 🙂

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Creation of a simple Turnover Analysis with Stories in People Analytics

Hello,
how the time interval can be trasformed to quarterly rate instead of annual rate?
Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP PostgreSQL Database, use as vectorstore database for AI Embeddings
entry.content: Good one Ali Mert! 🙂

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA readiness check: check failure
entry.content: That the namespace has nothing to do with the object in the check run is correct - that's what I tried to explain in point 3: In order to avoid inconsistencies, we do not check *any* SAP objects as soon as such an inconsistent namespace in the scope is detected, no matter whether there is actually an object from that namespace in the object set.

entry.title: In S/4 Hana Cloud, public ed. Renumber/Convert Ren. GL Accounts will also update transactinal data?
entry.content: In S/4 Hana Cloud, public ed., by using  Renumber/Convert Ren. GL Accounts the new G/L account IDs are written into the configuration tables, master data but also into transactional data already recorded in DEV system, in other words, documents already posted in DEV will be updated after the Renumber and Convert Ren.GL Accounts if the account on them are now renumber?

entry.title: Re: What to do when you forget to click 'Without Qty Post...
entry.content:
Canceling the document and creating a new one is the way to go.

Technically, you could just use Goods Receipt document to return the stock into warehouse without canceling credit note, but that wouldn't change the sales report (if that matters to you).

entry.title: 3 Compelling Reasons to Attend the Webinar on SAP Analytics Cloud's Integration with SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
As businesses increasingly rely on data to make informed decisions and drive growth, financial planning and analysis (FP&A) departments play a pivotal role in providing accurate and timely insights. To leverage the power of real-time analysis, data-driven narratives, and endless financial scenarios, attending a webinar on SAP Analytics Cloud's integration with SAP S/4HANA is a must for finance professionals. This blog post outlines three compelling reasons to participate in this informative session. (Registration Link)

1. Game-Changing Integration: SAP Rise and Grow Packaging with SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning
The webinar unveils how SAP Rise and Grow packaging, coupled with SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning, can revolutionize financial departments. Attendees will learn how this integrated approach streamlines financial planning processes, reduces complexity, and enhances cross-functional collaboration. By connecting data from SAP S/4HANA and leveraging the advanced analytics capabilities of SAP Analytics Cloud, finance professionals can unlock valuable insights and drive strategic decision-making.

2. Key Planning Capabilities Unveiled
Understanding the planning capabilities of any software is crucial for harnessing its full potential. In this webinar, participants will gain in-depth knowledge about SAP Analytics Cloud's planning capabilities. They will explore features such as budgeting, forecasting, and scenario modeling, enabling finance professionals to create comprehensive and accurate plans. Leveraging real-time data from SAP S/4HANA, participants will discover how to align financial plans with business objectives efficiently.

3. Rapid Suite Content for S/4HANA: Get Started Quickly
One of the challenges faced by finance departments when adopting new technologies is the implementation process. With Rapid Suite Content for S/4HANA, attendees will learn how to get started quickly and seamlessly integrate SAP Analytics Cloud into their organizations. This feature-rich content package offers pre-built models, KPIs, and reports tailored specifically for SAP S/4HANA Finance. By attending the webinar, finance professionals will gain insights into how to accelerate implementation and achieve immediate value with reduced time-to-insight.

Conclusion:
The integration between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP S/4HANA presents a transformative opportunity for FP&A departments to increase the effectiveness and efficiency of their financial planning processes. By attending the forthcoming webinar, participants will gain insights into the game-changing capabilities of SAP Rise and Grow packaging, understand the key planning capabilities, and learn how to quickly get started with Rapid Suite Content. (Registration Link)

entry.title: CPI URL not returning large payload
entry.content:
Hii,

I have consumed odata url (which have more than 1lakh data for a particular date range) in iflow with http adapter , after successfully generated CPI url then test it via postman with a particular date range(input parameters) and the API return Status Code 200ok but didn't return any data. it is happening when the payload is too large.

entry.title: Re: Diff Between Asset Class and Asset Group
entry.content: As you mentioned this point ---"In the FI-AA component, the calculation and posting of depreciation generally takes place at the level of the individual asset. The system is fundamentally conceived so that depreciation is calculated for each main number or sub-number. To meet certain tax reporting requirements (such as ADR in the United States), which require depreciation at a higher level than the individual asset (for example, all assets in a given class in a given vintage year)." Is there way where we can calculate the depreciation in higher level like for say at plant level?

entry.title: Solman configuration for EWA Report
entry.content:
Dear Team,

Please provide input while configuration of fresh system for EWA report for production server, what step needs to be follow.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Solman configuration for EWA Report
entry.content:
Hello

Please follow these KBAs to setup the EWA report generation:

1769513 - EWA not generated - session data is overdue (red flag status)

2729186 - General Process of EWA Generation in SAP Solution Manager 7.2

1257308 - FAQ: Using EarlyWatch Alert

 

2493440 - EWA Report troubleshooting and FAQ - Guided Answers --> Troubleshooting Guide

 

SAP Support

entry.title: CDS returns different result set if I query with filter
entry.content:
I have the following CDS view: 

define view entity ZCDSX_TEST as select from I_Equipment as e
left outer to one join I_SubscrpnContrItmRefObj as _refObjects on e.Equipment = _refObjects.SubscrpnContrItmRefObjEquip
{
    key e.Equipment as Equipment,   
    max(_refObjects.SubscriptionContract ) as ContractId,
    max(_refObjects.SubscriptionContractItem) as ContractItemId
}
group by e.Equipment, _refObjects.SubscriptionContract, _refObjects.SubscriptionContractItem

Where I can have more then one entry on the left outer join. I only want one entry which is why I'm using the max and group by. 

Let's say i query the whole set, I get 1 entry for equipment 1000001 despite the fact there are 10 entries for 100001 on the second table. 

Now if i do a query by primary key, inserting 1000001 in the "where" clause I get all 10 entries returned. 

Why do i have this behavior, with different results depending on the where clause? I can see this behavior both in SE16N, OData or ADT Data preview. The moment I set a filter for any column, the result set becomes different (the filter doesn't have to be on primary key, a filter on another column does the same thing).

Just as a note, I'm a newbie on this, this is more of a side project, just trying to understand CDS.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public edition Trial missing Communication Management tab
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Trial (14 days trial: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/s4hana/trial.html) to integrate with a SAP BTP trial account to try out an extension app scenario.

In the registration step 1 mentioned in https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/trigger-registration-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-tenant there is no Communication Management tab (and hence no "Maintain Extensions on SAP BTP" tile) in the SAP S/4HANA trial cloud tenant.

Is this not supported in the SAP S/4HANA trial account ? Are there any plans of enabling this so that one can try out extension scenarios or is there already an alternative?

Regards,

Mel

entry.title: Re: Create/Update listing exclusion in ECC using PRICAT I...
entry.content:
To create or update listing exclusions in SAP ECC using PRICAT inbound IDOC, you can follow these steps:

  • Configure PRICAT Inbound Process: Ensure that the PRICAT inbound process is configured in your SAP ECC system. This involves setting up the necessary partner profiles, message types, and processing routines for inbound PRICAT IDOCs.
  • Define Listing Exclusion Fields: Determine which fields in the PRICAT IDOC correspond to listing exclusions. These fields could include material numbers, vendor numbers, validity dates, or any other relevant attributes for listing exclusions.
  • Enhance PRICAT IDOC Structure: If the standard PRICAT IDOC structure doesn't already include fields for listing exclusions, you may need to enhance it using IDOC extension techniques such as adding custom segments or fields.
  • Map PRICAT Data to Listing Exclusions: Develop or configure the mapping logic to extract relevant data from the PRICAT IDOC and populate it into the appropriate fields for listing exclusions in SAP ECC.
  • Implement Business Logic: Define the business logic to determine when to create new listing exclusions or update existing ones based on the data received in the PRICAT IDOC. This may involve checking for existing listings, validating data against business rules, and triggering appropriate actions.
  • Error Handling: Implement error handling mechanisms to manage exceptions during the listing exclusion creation/update process. This includes handling validation errors, duplicate entries, and communication failures.
  • Testing: Thoroughly test the PRICAT inbound process with different scenarios to ensure that listing exclusions are created or updated correctly based on the incoming IDOC data.
  • Documentation and Training: Document the configuration, mapping rules, and business logic implemented for the PRICAT inbound process. Provide training to relevant stakeholders on how to monitor and troubleshoot listing exclusion-related issues.

entry.title: Table or T-code where Asset class can be found in SAP PM module?
entry.content:
Hi All,

Can anyone help me with T-code or table for Asset class column in SAP PM?

Requirement: I have an Asset class column in my SAP FI-AA Component (ANLA Table -- ANLKL column) which is numeric (data ex: 120076) so now I have to map the same to SAP PM module for calculation, but not sure in which table or T-code this Asset Class can be found in SAP PM module tables (ex: AUFK or EQUI or EQUZ or etc..) 

It would be a great help if anyone can provide the T-code or table.

Thanks in advance:)

entry.title: Re: Data Action Not Save
entry.content:
Hi

When i trying to go over from script to visual I get this message

Can you pls go over my script and tell if i have syntax error?

//check if the year is between the start date and the start date + duration of the contaract
 
IF INT(YEAR([d/Date])) = [d/LegalDoc].[p/DateStart] 
or INT(YEAR([d/Date])) <=([d/LegalDoc].[p/DateStart]+[d/LegalDoc].[p/TermDuration] )
 
THEN
//calculate the amount of the contract devided to the duration
   Data([d/Measures]="Changes",[d/IAA_GLACCOUNT]="522383200")= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="StartAmount",[d/Date]="2023")/ATTRIBUTE([d/LegalDoc].[p/TermDuration])
ENDIF
 
Thank you very much
 
@William_Yu 
@Nikhil1

entry.title: Multiple alerts for HDB version upgrade, etc. from SAP Alert Notification service for SAP BTP
entry.content:
We are receiving recurring multiple alert emails for HDB version upgrade and HDB patch upgrade, HDB end of maintenance like every 4 hours. We catch all alert notifications to notify for all events. Accordingly we receiving it. We do not want to hit the multiple recurring mails in our mail box. How to stop receiving these emails?

Thanks

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Multiple alerts for HDB version upgrade, etc. from SA...
entry.content:
Hello,

As you can see these emails come from the "SAP Alert Notification service for SAP BTP". Please refer to the official documentation of this SAP BTP Service on how to manage these actions:

3267020 - Alert Service for SAP HANA Cloud - SAP for Me

Alerts in SAP HANA Cloud | SAP Help Portal

Managing Actions | SAP Help Portal

Best Regards,

entry.title: Building Next Gen Product Managers @ SAP Labs India
entry.content:
Aspiring Product Management Curriculum at SAP Labs IndiaThe Product Management Forum at SAP Labs India is a vibrant hub that serves as a catalyst for collaboration, learning, and growth in the realm of Product Management. The forum has 3 primary initiatives with the objective of strengthening product thinking and taking PM learnings at SLI to the next level:

  1. Building a pool of end-to-end Product Managers (aimed for senior product managers
  2. Establishing Thought Leadership and External Community presence via knowledge sharing & gaining Outside-in perspective
  3. Grooming next generation product managers from the aspiring colleagues

I had the privilege of working closely on the third pillar, driving the “Aspiring Product Management” Curriculum. The Aspiring Product Management Curriculum is a part-time activity spread across 6 months with a program agenda that includes a combination of learning sessions, assignments, and sessions from external leaders across two phases.

The 6-months long curriculum was mainly divided into 2 phases: Phase 1 promoted an understating of SAP Big Picture by understanding SAP’s product and business strategy, diverse lines of business and industry applications at SAP, and Phase 2 focused on core Product Management principles ranging from Product Strategy and Portfolio planning to Go-to-Market and Product Design – thus promoting a holistic understanding of different verticals within the product management portfolio. The program also features assignments to implement and showcase all the learnings from the program.

This learning program aims at incorporating a gamified approach to keep participants engaged and motivated throughout the course of the program by incorporating game elements such as points, rewards, and leaderboards. Skill points are awarded for attending sessions and completing assignments, influencer points are earned by publishing blogs/articles/PoVs/external participation on the topics and learnings from the program, reputation points are earned by promoting outcomes on social media (Linkedin/Twitter) using predefined #tags, and karma points are given for supporting other team members and participating in volunteering projects.

Since the launch of the program, within the first 2 editions, the Aspiring Product Management program has garnered immensely positive feedback from over 120 colleagues who have successfully completed the program. The amazing feedback received from the participants is a testament to the fact that this program has indeed helped them to broaden their understanding of Product management function and principles. As we now embark on a 3rd edition of this program, it’s worthy to note that several colleagues from the past editions of the program have successfully transitioned to Product Management roles within SAP and others are now more confident with the freshly inculcated product mindset.

A program of such scale and successful outcome would certainly not be possible without the great leadership that we have in the SVP & MD, SAP Labs India Sindhu Gangadharan . And certainly not without the able guidance, support and encouragement from PavanKumar PVN and Deepak Padmanabha  who helped conceptualise and drive the very first instance of the program!

Justin Paul Shiva Kumar H S Ajay Nayak

#productmanagement #productthinking #aspiringPM #PMcurriculum

entry.title: Re: sap hana certification
entry.content: Thanks Margit .. Can you please share what are all the corresponding learning journeys for this certification course? some of my interns are preparing for the C_HANAIMP exam and not sure which learning journeys to study for this new certification course

entry.title: Re: Field KOMV-KSCHL (13) does not exist in dynpro SAPMV4...
entry.content:
Yes. You are right...
The issue occurs for those with a smaller screen size...
Could you help by proposing a fix?
Like, can we change the SAP screen size and resolution?

Thanks 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP JAVA accessing business rules destination is...
entry.content:
I'm a developer from the SAP Cloud SDK team.

The error message "The destination service responded with an error: 'Bad credentials'" indicates that the BTP Destination Service was unable to resolve an access token with the OAuth2 Client Credentials grant. That means the request you ran with Postman does not work for the service respectively.

SAP Cloud SDK is not performing the token exchange/retrieval operation. Instead it fully relies on BTP Destination Service. However the SDK caches the service response for a few minutes.

Does the problem persist? If yes, then I would rather recommend approaching the BTP Destination Service colleagues.

entry.title: How to create authentication(Sign up and login page) page in sap build apps ?
entry.content: I am creating an app using sap build apps. How can i create register and login page? In appgyver we have firebase. But for build apps is there any way?

entry.content: Thank you, Sandra Rossi... We will be implementing your proposed solution.. Thanks a lot 🙂

IMG activity where you enter your condition table layout does create your tables also local in the test system. its not possible to delete the table and transport bc its also local thenin test system.

entry.title: Re: QA33 NO LAYOUTS FOUND
entry.content: Take the inputs from SAP note 840321

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP Java connection to OnPremise destination serv...
entry.content: Please check whether you actually have an SAP Cloud Connector configured in your CF account that's able to serve route http://devsage:9999

entry.title: Building Next Gen Product Managers @ SAP Labs India
entry.content:
Aspiring Product Management Curriculum at SAP Labs IndiaThe Product Management Forum at SAP Labs India is a vibrant hub that serves as a catalyst for collaboration, learning, and growth in the realm of Product Management. The forum has 3 primary initiatives with the objective of strengthening product thinking and taking PM learnings at SLI to the next level:

  1. Building a pool of end-to-end Product Managers (aimed for senior product managers
  2. Establishing Thought Leadership and External Community presence via knowledge sharing & gaining Outside-in perspective
  3. Grooming next generation product managers from the aspiring colleagues

I had the privilege of working closely on the third pillar, driving the “Aspiring Product Management” Curriculum. The Aspiring Product Management Curriculum is a part-time activity spread across 6 months with a program agenda that includes a combination of learning sessions, assignments, and sessions from external leaders across two phases.

The 6-months long curriculum was mainly divided into 2 phases: Phase 1 promoted an understating of SAP Big Picture by understanding SAP’s product and business strategy, diverse lines of business and industry applications at SAP, and Phase 2 focused on core Product Management principles ranging from Product Strategy and Portfolio planning to Go-to-Market and Product Design – thus promoting a holistic understanding of different verticals within the product management portfolio. The program also features assignments to implement and showcase all the learnings from the program.

This learning program aims at incorporating a gamified approach to keep participants engaged and motivated throughout the course of the program by incorporating game elements such as points, rewards, and leaderboards. Skill points are awarded for attending sessions and completing assignments, influencer points are earned by publishing blogs/articles/PoVs/external participation on the topics and learnings from the program, reputation points are earned by promoting outcomes on social media (Linkedin/Twitter) using predefined #tags, and karma points are given for supporting other team members and participating in volunteering projects.

Since the launch of the program, within the first 2 editions, the Aspiring Product Management program has garnered immensely positive feedback from over 120 colleagues who have successfully completed the program. The amazing feedback received from the participants is a testament to the fact that this program has indeed helped them to broaden their understanding of Product management function and principles. As we now embark on a 3rd edition of this program, it’s worthy to note that several colleagues from the past editions of the program have successfully transitioned to Product Management roles within SAP and others are now more confident with the freshly inculcated product mindset.

A program of such scale and successful outcome would certainly not be possible without the great leadership that we have in the SVP & MD, SAP Labs India Sindhu Gangadharan . And certainly not without the able guidance, support and encouragement from PavanKumar PVN and Deepak Padmanabha  who helped conceptualise and drive the very first instance of the program!

Justin Paul Shiva Kumar H S Ajay Nayak

#productmanagement #productthinking #aspiringPM #PMcurriculum

entry.title: Re: Create DB table based on CDS's structure.
entry.content: Give the name of your own table to DD FM not the CDS view name. (The table you "Create a table in SE11")

entry.title: Re: Hide Save Button (BTP, RAP)
entry.content:
@sebastian_wilhelm1 

Could you tell me why you want to hide the save button? If it's for a read-only scenario, you can achieve it simply by not creating a Behavior Definition.

entry.title: Re: where to check administrative data - Account Group in...
entry.content: It would be in Customer General Data tab as shown below

entry.title: Re: sap hana certification
entry.content:
Hi @Giridharan_S 
Please check the following site SAP Certified Application Associate - SAP HANA 2.0 SPS06 where you can find among other information under How to prepare the leaning journey. 
However this certification will be retired on 2024-03-28.
You can find more details on our Our  Certification Transformation site which also includes the link to the SAP Certification Recommendation document 
In this document you can find the recommendation for recertification: C_HANAIMP will be renamed to C_HAMOD. 
No re-certification required for those already certified in C_HANAIMP.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Addressing Common Questions and Complaints About the New Platform
entry.content:
Hi @jerryjanda ,
crucial functionality for me is to search posts with keywords, limited by tag(s).
For example, I would like to see all posts with phrase "dashboard" on SAP Managed Tag "C4C Analytics".
Example 2. I would like to see all posts with phrase "accounts not replicating", tagged as "C4C Sales" OR "C4C Service".

Is this functionality on your roadmap?

Best regards,
Adam

entry.title: Re: augment_update Method not called
entry.content: The augment methods are called before the save routine - after pressing the "Save" or "Create" button on the object page. And only, if there has been changes on the BO

While creating the vendor in vendor group I am getting the error like below 

Allocate last number of the interval

 

Please answer my query

entry.title: Re: Hide Save Button (BTP, RAP)
entry.content:
Hi @MioYasuatke! Thanks for you reply.

In my App, a user can maintain article data (ad-hoc) and by pressing a button "Post Goods Movement" an OData Servie on an on-premise system triggers the Post Goods Movement Process. So, I don't want to save any data on the DB. Therefore there must no "Save" Button. Or is there another way, to create a transactional app without the need of saving data?

I'm working with a managed Scenario with OData V4).

entry.title: Re: Integration of a DM Side-by-Side Extension into Fiori...
entry.content:
Hi,
It's currently not possible to manually add custom tiles to external apps to DM Launchpad.
Alternatively, you can evaluate if using SAP Launchpad Service in BTP to mix and match DM apps and your custom tiles would fullfil your requirements.
Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: "Error calling pricing service layer (method SAVE)" when trying subscription to sales order
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to configure to create sales order from Subscription order. I created Sales order with name and number range same as subscription order. Also in Data exchange in service class = CL_CRMS4_SOM_SALESDOC_FWD and Transaction Cat = BUS2000265.

While trying to create subscription order in fiori, i am getting following error:

Message class - CRMS4_PRC_ADAPTER, Message number - 007, Message Text - Error calling pricing service layer (method SAVE).

Can someone please help.

entry.title: Re: AI/ML apps and Visual inspection APIs Deprecated
entry.content:
Hi,

The new AI/ML Visual Inspection is currently in BETA. GA is planned for 24.05 release in May, learn more at: 

3419039 - Additional Information and Manual Activities for SAP Digital Manufacturing, Release 2402
3435403 - Additional Information and Manual Activities for SAP Digital Manufacturing, Release 2405

AI-driven Visual Inspections (BETA), powered by SAP Digital Manufacturing 
Video: https://buff.ly/3uTmDib
IVJ: https://buff.ly/3uRP7ZE

The new APIs can be found in API Business Hub at: https://api.sap.com/api/sapdme_vimodel/resource/InspectionResults

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: C4C V2 Rest API - Account master data
entry.content:
Hi community,

we are transforming to C4C V2 and have to switch our integration with datasphere from oData to Rest API now. So far I'm aware of the reference https://api.sap.com/package/SAPSalesServiceCloudV2/rest and here especially the opportunity service (https://api.sap.com/api/SalesSvcCloudV2_opportunity/overview). Here I also find the account ID and name. But I need more of the account master data like e.g. information about the industry of an account. 

Which service can I use, to get this additional master data information of accounts?

thanks in advance for your feedback and support.

Best Regards - Christian

Kind regards

Grace Nesuwo

entry.title: Re: SAP Direct Distribution - A New Mobile App for Direct Store Delivery
entry.content:
Hi @NaumanAH,

Thanks for your feedback! Indeed, a limited number of printers were supported within the “old” DSD mobile solution and it is not planned to provide additional drivers for this solution anymore.

Anyhow, we are currently working on the printer integration for the NEW version of SDD Mobile based on S/4HANA. We'll provide more information about default printer integrations and extensibility options as soon as it is released. We'll consider your feedback regarding this and keep you and the community posted on the implementation concepts.

Best regards,
SDD Mobile Team

entry.title: Re: Employee Central Global Benefits enrollment using Intelligent Services Center (ISC)
entry.content:
Hi!

Can you advise where and how to change the BEN enrollment workflow approvers after hiring?

Or if the workflow itself can be removed?

entry.title: Re: Some of the transactions are taking more time from DB...
entry.content: Hi Sandra Check this SAP note 2524975 Performance tracing overview: SAT, ST05 & ST12 traces

entry.title: Re: Line Monitor
entry.content:
Hi @patwage , if my answer was helpful, please consider accepting it as the solution.

Thanks,
Manoel

entry.title: Custom fields are now available for "Manage Customer Line Items" and "Manage Supplier Line Items"
entry.content:
You may know our blog post Need another field in Manage Customer Line Items or Manage Supplier Line Items?, in which we showed you how you can use data source extension to add missing fields to your apps.
With data source extension, however, you can only add fields that SAP delivers as additional fields for a data source. But what if you need a completely different field?

Well, there is good news: as of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402, you can create your own fields, also known as custom fields, and use them in the Manage Customer Line Items app or the Manage Supplier Line Items app. You just need to  create your field in the Accounting: Journal Entry Item business context and enable usage of the field in the Manage Customer Line Items app or the Manage Supplier Line Items app. For more information on how to do this, see our Product Assistance on How to Enable Custom Fields.

Want to know more?

If you’re looking for a more detailed example and use case for custom fields, check out our Custom fields for the "Manage Customer Line Items" app: use case blog post.

Thanks for reading this blog post. We hope that you’re just as excited as we are that you can now use custom fields to enhance the Manage Customer Line Items app or the Manage Supplier Line Items app.

Feel free to leave any comments you may have below.

For more information about the apps, see:
Manage Customer Line Items
Manage Supplier Line Items

entry.title: Custom fields for the "Manage Customer Line Items" app: use case
entry.content:
You may have read our blog post Custom fields are now available for "Manage Customer Line Items" and "Manage Supplier Line Items", in which we introduced custom fields for the Manage Customer Line Items app and the Manage Supplier Line Items app. In this blog post, we’d like to give you a more detailed example and use case for custom fields.

Let’s assume you want to display profit center information in the Manage Customer Line Items app. As explained in our blog post on How to Display Profit Center Assignments for AR/AP Line Items, you may need this because the Manage Customer Line Items app usually shows exactly one line item per invoice, but revenue postings can be distributed over different profit centers. You would see this as separate line items in the Manage Journal Entries app.

In our example, you want to display the profit center with the highest amount in the Responsible Unit custom field in the Manage Customer Line Items app. To do so, you must simply follow this five-step procedure:

Step 1: Create a custom field

You open the Custom Fields app and create the Responsible Unit custom field in the Accounting: Journal Entry Item business context.
The following screenshot shows the dialog box in which you enter your details to create the custom field:


Step 2: Enable field usage

You then enable the field for the Manage Customer Line Items app and publish your custom field. For more information, see How to Enable Custom Fields.
The following screenshot shows the search result for Customer Line Item on the User Interface tab. The Enable Usage column is highlighted.

 

The following screenshot shows that the field usage has been enabled in the OData service and the related usage in the relevant CDS view:


The custom field has now been defined and is available in the Manage Customer Line Items app. The following screenshot shows the field in the Settings dialog box.


Step 3: Create and publish the custom logic to fill the Responsible Unit custom field

To set a value in the newly-created custom field, you can implement a custom logic that determines a value for the custom field.
To do so, you open the Custom Logic app to create your implementation of the Journal Entry Substitution (FIN_ACDOC_SUBSTITUTION) extension point. For more information, see Creating Implementations.
The following screenshot shows the extension point search result:



Step 4: Write custom code

Once you’ve published the implementation, you can write your custom code.
The following code snippet gives you an idea of how you can define which data you want to display in your Responsible Unit custom field in the Manage Customer Line Items app. Note that this code snippet is for demonstration purposes only.
In our example, you want to display the profit center from the G/L line item that has the amount.

Step 5: Test custom field in the Manage Customer Line Items app

This new custom logic is applied to all new and changed items only. To test the custom logic, simply select an existing line item, use the Edit Line Item function, and change the item.


Thanks for reading this blog post. We hope that it gave you an idea of how you can use custom fields to enhance the Manage Customer Line Items app. The same is possible for the Manage Supplier Line Items app, too.
Feel free to leave any comments you may have below.

For more information about the apps, see:
Manage Customer Line Items
Manage Supplier Line Items

entry.title: How to add new selection fields in MSR_TRC_C - Tracking of Customer Returns
entry.content:
Dear Guru,

We are implementing SAP ARM solution in Italy in an S/4Hana system on premise, our customer required to add some new selection fields in the T-Code MSR_TRC_C - Tracking of Customer Returns but till now we could not find any enhancement in the related program. Do you have any insight about how to enhance this T-Code?

Thanks in advance for helping

Maria

entry.title: Withholding tax in I_WITHHOLDINGTAXITEM not the same in Display Line Items in General Ledger
entry.content:
There is  a discrepancy in withholding tax between the app Display Line Items in General Ledger (App ID F2217) and CDS View (Withholding Tax Amount in Local Currency) I_WITHHOLDINGTAXITEM 

Upon review, there is a mismatch between the expected credit balance in the GL and the displayed debit balance in the report.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Withholding tax in I_WITHHOLDINGTAXITEM not the same...
entry.content: See kba 3228045 Extended withholding tax with line item display - Cloud which outlines the standard design

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner – Address Management at Contact Persons
entry.content: Awesome post Andi! Very informative and helpful for master data operations!

entry.title: Re: Upgrading to Latest Goal Management – Cycle Planning & Preparedness – Best Practice Approach
entry.content: We have upgraded to latest goal mgmt in Preview, and I have converted some of our prior year's templates so that users can copy from other plan. I get an error when trying to convert our current template (group goals), but I don't recall we ever had group goals. We have not upgraded PRD yet, and I want to make sure that I am able to convert the current template for when we do upgrade. What do I need to do to ensure I can convert once we do upgrade PRD?
